mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 318d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 318d engine typically costs £2,500–£5,800 supply only depending on generation, with the current B47D20 sitting at £3,400–£5,800. Fitting adds £650–£1,300. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which of the three engine generations your 318d has — this badge spans the M47 (2001–2005, excellent reliability, no chain risk), the N47 (2005–2011, the well-documented rear-chain risk generation), and the B47 (2011–present, chain relocated to the front, meaningfully improved).

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 318d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47-engined cars, swirl flap failure on the M47 150bhp variant and early N47s, and an EGR cooler coolant leak affecting both N47 and B47 F30-era cars, subject to a BMW recall.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 318d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. Against a well-kept £7,000 318d, a £4,000 recon is 57% of the car's value — approaching our 70% BMW threshold. Against a tired £3,500 example, the same recon exceeds our threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 318d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E46 (2001–2005) is M47, E90 (2005–2011) is N47, F30/G20 (2011–present) is B47. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,000–£5,200 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

eraMap.title: The 318d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: BMW's durable early diesel — no rear-chain risk. Swirl flaps the only real weak point.

rows.reliability: ★★★☆☆ 1,450 JLR-wide [BMW-VERIFIED — inherited from Model Page & E90 Generation Page]

rows.eraNote: Chain relocated to the front — a genuinely different risk profile.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current G20/B47 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E46/M47 and E90/N47-specific pricing, which runs lower.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 318d engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: N47 Rear Timing Chain Failure (E90, early F30)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle lasting 1–30 seconds, metallic ticking, engine warning light.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: This is the single most searched 318d issue for good reason — at the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected. A £650–£1,800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,000+ replacement that follows. The 318d shares the N47's rear-mounted chain design across E90 and early F30 models.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Swirl Flap Failure (M47 150bhp variant, E46)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, occasional rattling from the intake.
problems.replacementCost: £850–£1,200 (intake manifold, or engine repair if debris entered a cylinder).
problems.recommendation: This affects the M47's 150bhp common-rail variant and remains a known issue on early N47s too. Preventive blanking is cheap, effective, and removes the risk entirely with no noticeable performance loss.

failureLink.label: Read the full swirl flap failure guide

problems.title: EGR Cooler Coolant Leak (N47 and B47, all F30-era)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white exhaust smoke, occasional burning smell.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — this was the subject of a global BMW recall affecting both N47 and B47 diesel variants.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — this doesn't typically progress to engine replacement if addressed, but ignored coolant loss can lead to overheating damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: check recall completion status on any F30-era 318d.

problems.recommendation: Verify recall completion before buying any N47 or B47-engined F30 318d — this is a straightforward, well-documented fix when caught early.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before it becomes catastrophic
Blanking plates remove the risk entirely
Straightforward fix if caught before overheating
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Viable on lower-mileage examples; a lottery above 250,000km

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 318d? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing-chain history if buying an E90 or pre-LCI F30
* Confirmed EGR recall completion on any F30-era car
* Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
* A B47 if you want the cleanest slate of any 318d generation
avoidIf: * E90 or pre-LCI F30 with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* No proof of swirl flap blanking or replacement on an M47 150bhp or early N47
* Poor service history on any generation — this badge is unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of generation

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: Across three engine generations of this badge, the honest advice barely changes: buy on documented history, not on generation alone. The M47 and B47 are this badge's two genuinely low-risk eras; the E90 N47 demands real chain-history proof before you commit.

cta.label: → Compare 318d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 318d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for 1–30 seconds of rattle (timing chain warning, N47/E90)
* Service History — including which engine era (M47/N47/B47) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak
* Turbo Boost — Test acceleration; any lag, whistling, or grinding?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
